programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is murdered at the home of Gunnery Sergeant Holt Perkins, and while the victim is initially assumed to be the marine's wife, analysis reveals her to be a convicted thief, presumed to be on the scene as part of a plot to hit Perkins' house. When the agents discover the calling card of notorious terror cell Dragon's Tooth on the body, it becomes clear the murder has been committed as part of a wider agenda, and the team joins forces with the Department of Defence to catch the killer. American drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two. Hayes Cooper joins the ranks of the Texas Rangers when he sets out to avenge the deaths of his friends. Starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Crime drama spin-off, starring Patricia Arquette as Special Agent Avery Ryan, head of the FBI's cyber crime division. The team's first assignment involves a kidnapped baby and as Ryan and forensics expert Elijah Mundo investigate the clues they discover that whoever was responsible hacked into a baby monitor to establish when the child was alone and vulnerable. Also starring James Van Der Beek

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. Hetty dispatches the team to recover a book containing top-secret information, which has been stolen from the personal effects of a murdered antiques dealer. Starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.desc: Joseph's suspicions increase after Faye is again questioned by police over her husband's death. programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Simon's shameless flirting with a new arrival to the department infuriates Colette, whose problems increase tenfold when a well-intentioned surprise backfires, while Josh remains blissfully unaware of his wife's thoughts about their marriage. Lara is shocked to discover the reasons for a baby's mysterious symptoms and Harry resorts to blackmail to save a patient's arm. Anna has a dismal blind date, which turns out to be a blessing in disguise, while Ryan asks Duffy a big question. Drama, starring Christopher Colquhoun, Adjoa Andoh and Ian Bleasdale

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lewis plays midwife to a road accident victim who goes into labour, while Roger searches her car for an overnight bag and discovers a large haul of cocaine - some of which is later found in her bloodstream. programme.desc: The doctor is summoned by farmer's wife Mrs Henderson, who presents him with a puzzling case to solve involving her son, while Dr Cameron moves to his country cottage

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Conclusion of a tense two-part story. As Cardale struggles to come to terms with David's shocking death, a destructive patient pushes Andrew's patience to the limit. Long-running medical drama, starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne and Fiona Gillies

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: A raid on a farm believed to be harbouring illegal immigrants gets out of hand, leaving Paige to pick up the pieces. Drama, starring Nigel Havers and Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William and Hester disagree over whether to cut property costs by moving to a smaller home, while Guy makes romantic overtures to his estranged wife. Comedy, starring Anton Rodgers, Julia McKenzie and Ballard Berkeley

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Financial problems force Diana to share a flat with Tom, and a new resident moves into Bayview. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene attempts to send an urgent message to London via the transmitter under his twin brother's tomb - but ends up getting carried away. Wartime comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Carmen Silvera and Vicki Michelle

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Truly investigates the case of a bus that disappeared between stops. Whimsical comedy, starring Frank Thornton, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Kavanagh QC
programme.desc: A single mother from Sunderland asks Kavanagh to prosecute the neighbourhood vigilante she suspects of attempting to murder her teenage tearaway son in a hit-and-run incident. At first he has misgivings about accepting a case so far from home, but finally agrees _ only to find his already strained family life suffering as a result. Tense legal drama, starring John Thaw and Lisa Harrow, with a guest appearance by Robert Glenister

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Fresh DNA tests on an unidentified body found in 1996 reveal a family link to a Home Office fingerprint analyst, who has not seen her brother since she was seven, when he brought her over to the UK from their home country of Albania. The victim had been working as a police interpreter - and the motive seems clear when it turns out his last assignment was translating for a witness in a murder trial against a notorious Albanian criminal. Meanwhile, Gerry embarks on a French cookery course, but the language barrier results in a rather surprising date. Amanda Redman and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: Reports of extremist activity at a Birmingham mosque alert the team to the existence of suicide bomber training. When one MI5 agent is exposed as an infiltrator and tortured, help arrives in the form of an Algerian spy. But when a terrorist outrage is planned, it becomes a race against time to identify the target before his cover is also blown. Guest starring Alexander Siddig, best known as Dr Bashir in Star Trek: Deep Space Nine, with Matthew Macfadyen and Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An accident involving a lorry and a funeral procession results in the bizarre discovery of two bodies in the coffin. Meanwhile, Leo and Nikki's suspicions are aroused during a post mortem on a child who died in surgery, and both investigations lead the detectives to a surgeon with a high rate of mortality among her patients. Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and Natasha Little star

programme.title: The Bill

Terry is not best pleased to learn his daughter is dating a copper and Neil Manson sends suspected rapist Keith Durante away with nothing more than a warning because there is no evidence he is stalking nurse Beth Adamson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Will Fletcher is assigned to investigate a secret organisation of racist thugs planning to strike at the heart of the Muslim community. Emma confesses to Matt she found the engagement ring in his desk drawer and seeks solace with Will when her long-term boyfriend reacts badly to rejection

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: The arrival of new locum Dr Sam Morgan at the Beeches sets female hearts a-flutter in the village, while Joanna's heroic efforts to arrange a much-needed operation for an Alzheimer's sufferer bring her into contact with an old flame. Andrew stirs up resentment by ordering receptionist Dawn Rudge to give up her other job. Rural drama, starring Gary Mavers, Haydn Gwynne, Joseph Millson and John Alderton

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: DI Gillian Cramer's ex-husband approaches her for help when his force comes under investigation by Internal Affairs

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: Hestor is on edge as unmarried daughter Emma announces she's expecting a baby and news reaches her that a food critic will be attending Lucy's kitchen during her shift. Julia McKenzie stars

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: New resident Daisy tries to instil some discipline, but Diana is worried about her intentions toward Tom. Comedy, starring Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Rene learns that the Gestapo are plotting to disguise themselves as British airmen - prompting General Von Klinkerhoffen to adopt a similar plan for Captain Bertorelli's soldiers. Later, the cafe owner and his workers dress up as characters from horror films to fool Herr Flick

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Smiler goes on the run when he has a tipple too much of Auntie's home-made wine, leaving Compo, Truly and Barry to track him down. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Peter Sallis stars

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: When a man dies at a cash machine and two teenage girls confess to the murder of a taxi driver, the detective appears to have two open-and-shut cases. However, Wallander's investigation leads him to discover a conspiracy that stretches far beyond the borders of Sweden. Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh, Sarah Smart and David Warner

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: New information links the unexplained death of a market trader to a series of drug rapes in east London - but with that case still ongoing, Ucos officers are unable to question the chief suspect, so they have to look into their victim's past in their search for clues. Gerry finds a likely culprit in the shape of the market inspector who clamped his car, while Sandra has problems of a more personal nature when her hated mother arrives for a visit. Sheila Hancock guest stars

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: A teacher is suspected of having links to a violent socialist group and hacking into MI5 files, causing agents to be lost in the field. Zoe goes undercover at his school to find out about him, but the more she learns, the less she believes he is the one responsible. Keeley Hawes stars, with Christopher Fairbank and William Gaminara

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A horrific car crash poses troubling questions for Nikki when she discovers one of the victims was the recently released Anna Holland, an accomplice to murder she helped escape a life sentence four years earlier. As the investigation progresses, Nikki re-interviews key witnesses from the original case and begins to doubt her earlier conclusions. Emilia Fox stars

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paige finds himself giving evidence against a former patient in a murder trial, but when the accused realises who he is, a straightforward case becomes more complex

programme.title: Fresh Fields
programme.desc: William organises a special getaway to mark their 24th wedding anniversary. He surprises Hester by taking her back to the hotel where he first proposed - but an even bigger surprise is waiting for them when they get there

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Tom and Diana investigate a spate of mysterious thefts at Bayview, and Tom ends up facing a shoplifting charge - but Diana decides to defend him

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Von Klinkerhoffen's trust for the Gestapo is rapidly diminishing, so he orders Helga to marry Herr Flick so she can keep an eye on his activities. Elsewhere, the British airmen head to the canal to rendezvous with the submarine waiting for them. Comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Hilary Minster and Kim Hartman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Barry comes to Marina's rescue after a nasty bike accident - only to spark rumours of marital disharmony. However, Truly is quick to suggest a remedy for the gossip with the help of borrowed lipstick. Comedy, with Bill Owen, Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuth must stop a much-loved institution from going off the rails when the local stationmaster is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: The stalking of a TV game show host seems an ideal case for trainee detective Kyprianou to cut his teeth on, but Maisie soon regrets her decision to hand him the investigation. Police drama, starring Pauline Quirke and Steve John Shepherd

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: MI5 are called in when a family-owned bank is robbed to the tune of $1billion. As the team races to find the missing money and the culprit, undercover agent Danny discovers the firm has many dark secrets, and that someone in the highest echelons of the British Government is making sure they do not get out. Guest starring Robert Hardy, Oliver Ford Davies, Julie Cox and Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki investigates the discovery of human bones on a former traveller site which could be the remains of a senior police officer's missing teenage daughter. However, her forensic analysis raises even greater questions and casts doubt on the guilt of the chief suspect. Meanwhile, Harry is assigned to an insurance case that takes a peculiar twist. Emilia Fox and Tom Ward star

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Heaton and Meadows argue about the wisdom of using an inexperienced officer in undercover surveillance, Will is lured into a trap and ends up in a fight at the community centre, leaving Emma wondering whether she knows him at all. Mickey Webb rushes to the rescue as the timer of a bomb planted by Euan Thomas runs down at the NSAB headquarters. Matt Hinckley persuades Emma to marry him

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two terraces near beautiful countryside in Prestatyn in north Wales and Darley Dale in the Peak District and a shop and flat in Folkestone in Kent are all sold under the hammer. All three properties need a lot of work and Martin Roberts, Dion Sublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new investors to hear their plans

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: National Crime Agency officers seize cash and drugs from a suspected drug dealer, and Border Force officers at Gatwick sell off a huge haul of gold seized from a passenger

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright heads to Liverpool where council housing officers deal with the case of a family who are struggling to cope with a landlord they claim is intrusive

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall give advice to two teams scouring Ardingly in West Sussex for items to sell at auction, where one of Willy Wonka's golden tickets goes under the hammer

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie discovers more about the piano player she is in love with. Joyce realises there is something wrong with Martin and decides to follow him. Elsewhere, Finch helps Esther raise money by setting up a boxing match with his old adversary Vernon. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Susan Cookson and Mykola Allen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a calligrapher from East London find a new home in Devon on a budget of £400,000, and attends a sea shanty festival on the southern coast

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich set to work transforming a garden in Bitterne, near Southampton, for Katie and Christian and their two children, Max and Olivia

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three treasured family heirlooms - a wooden toolbox, a plate camera and a marionette theatre

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood examines bogus Nectar cards, and fake fire resistant glass that was installed in schools, hospitals and churches around the UK

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail forges a connection with Adil, a new visitor to base camp, and pushes a devastated Raelle to help him save a life. Tally uses Gerit as a distraction from her increasingly burdensome secrets, while Anacostia probes into Scylla's past

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The witches' training culminates at City Drop, their final training exercise. Devastating secrets come to light, splintering the unit and forcing Tally to question her blind trust. Later, a looming Spree threat changes the course of the cadets' lives

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace consider some of the very best highlights from the show, from the most amazing dishes to outstanding challenges to some of the most impressive individual success stories. John and Gregg also reflect on what it takes to be 'the best', and how finalists and winners start to shine during the culinary rounds - in particular when in 2012 the semi-finalists took over the brasserie on a cross-channel ferry. Then the judges revisit one of the final challenges from 2008, when three celebrities faced cooking with Marcus Wareing - which resulted in one of the most memorable moments in Celebrity MasterChef history

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wallasey on Merseyside, Porth in south Wales, and Ripley in Derbyshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A criminal has his Rolex watch seized and the police target counterfeit cigarette sellers in Cleveland, believing large sums of money are being made from unsafe products

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: The housing officers in Liverpool visit a cramped terraced property in a worrying state of repair. In Birmingham, an overgrown property is in danger of taking over the neighbours

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Catherine Southon help teams at Sandown Park Racecourse in Surrey find items of value. Presenter Eric Knowles also visits Milestones Museum in Basingstoke

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie fears a young evacuee is in danger, while a death leaves Joyce reeling. Lady Ellen alarms her jealous sister when she shows her warm side, and Jack tries to persuade Bea that Chicago is the safest place for her and the baby. Esther decides to take drastic action to raise the money needed to save Martin's eyesight

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple returning from 20 years living in America as they look for a home in Hampshire's New Forest near to their children's new school. Sonali also goes behind the scenes during the preparations for a hugely popular traditional country show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete for the chance to transform the Romsey garden of Harry and Anna, turning it from a boring blank patch into a sophisticated party space

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on a surveyors' theodolite dating back 100 years, a brass bell from a Victorian ice-cream business, and a 60-year-old leather football. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show with Alex Jones and Ronan Keating, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle goes to the seaside cafe to ring Kheerat only to get a shock when she returns to the B&B. Ian tightens the reins on Sharon running the Vic, and Callum asks Ben if the offer to move in together is still on the table - but their happy moment is short-lived. Stuart and Rainie prepare for their stag and hen parties, although things do not exactly go to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for the cops on the island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. However, it's all change for the team. DI Jack Mooney (Ardal O'Hanlon) is now in charge, and his first Caribbean case sees the reopening of a seven-year-old murder when a tourist provides an alibi for the woman convicted of the crime

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view a chalet bungalow in East Peckham, Kent, a piece of land in Tredegar, south-east Wales, and an end-terrace in Hartshill, Warwickshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in North Yorkshire look for county lines drug dealers in Harrogate, while at the auction one fraudster is about to have their home sold to pay back the money they made illegally. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates a Surrey property overwhelmed with rubbish and talks to the owner about their struggles with hoarding. In west London, probate genealogist Richard tracks down a deceased homeowner's relatives to help them claim their inheritance

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie tries to win Henry's respect by standing up to the overbearing bishop, and Finch is angry to learn Esther received money for Martin's operation. Fed up with the Land Army, Joyce forces Tucker into an admission of guilt, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana is part of Jack's plan to escape with Bea and the baby. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Liam Garrigan and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes from their favourite cookery books, explaining their value as social documents that reveal a great deal about people and what they ate. The duo meet Ivan Day, who has one of the most extensive collections of recipe books in the UK, and share stories of renowned food writers such as Elizabeth David and Isabella Beeton

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Precious metal expert Brenton West takes on a pewter chess set representing the War of Independence, complete with 32 figures of British and American soldiers. A Syrian chair and a wartime mascot doll are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Riyadh Khalaf host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Chantelle arrive back in Walford, but despite her finally finding the courage to tell her husband she is leaving him, Chantelle is unaware that the silent horror she has lived with for many years is about to get even worse

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A hypnotist is booked to provide the entertainment at the triplets' baptism - and Agnes soon finds herself under his spell. Meanwhile, the matriarch's kitchen is falling apart - but that turns out to be the least of her problems when son Mark delivers some heartbreaking news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Adventure, recounted through Tonto's memories of the earliest efforts from himself and the Lone Ranger to subdue local villainy and bring justice to the American Old West. Johnny Depp, Armie Hammer, Tom Wilkinson and Helena Bonham Carter star

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Experts restore a tarnished rugby trophy, a hand-operated Victorian sewing machine, and a 1930s accordion, which has 64 individual components in need of repair

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to transform a Reading garden to make it safe for children - despite the fact that no landscaper has been willing to take on the broken slate and rotten decking

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: A Yorkshire hill farming family that keeps sheep and cows diversifies by investing in 300 goats, which are sold not just for meat and dairy products, but also goat milk soap. In the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair settle into their new farm while also awaiting the birth of their first child - but a mix-up with the measuring tape proves costly and an escapee calf causes havoc. Crofter Graham is forced to adapt his working routine after a serious injury, and a storm threatens a Northumberland couple's plans to build a new home for their prize bull

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Dorset as driving instructor Helen Adams and former flight attendant Yvonne Johnson use their own cash to buy and sell antiques to try to make the most profit

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: The consequences of Henry's accident brings soul searching for some. Meanwhile, Anna arrives back in Sydney with some exciting news from Hollywood

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Drama-documentary re-creating biologist Joe Hutto's year spent raising 13 wild turkeys from an endangered breed in Florida. The scientist looked after the birds as though they were his own children, learning to communicate with them and seeing the world from their perspective, before experiencing a sense of heartache as they left him to begin life on their own

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: An insight into the largely unexplored Southern Red Sea, which is teeming with marine life and home to some of the warmest waters on the planet

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Will Axon and Christina Trevanion assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, a 17th-century Palladian house owned by the Marquess of Hertford. The pair unearth treasures to take off to auction including a Polynesian paddle and an 18th-century drinking glass. Presenter Paul Martin also explores the work of famous sculptor and wood carver Grinling Gibbons

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: At the Speciality and Fine Food Fair in London, potato farmer Anthony Froggatt hopes to attract interest in his premium crisp products made on his farm in Staffordshire, while 'farmers of the sea' Alison and David Lea-Wilson promote their salt, harvested from Anglesey, north Wales. Narrated by Bill Paterson

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actors Felicity Montagu and Clare Holman search around the Midlands for antiques to make a profit at auction, with the help of experts James Braxton and Arusha Irvine. The pair uncover an exceptional lampshade and snooker cue stand, before taking a trip in the classic car to explore more of Britain's rich history

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the second episode, Mary takes a journey along the Thames in London to discover culinary hidden gems that inspire her to make some deliciously comforting dishes. Her journey starts with the Henley Rowing Club, where she rustles up mixed bean and butternut squash wraps for the team, before heading to The Waterside Inn, the world famous three Michelin Star restaurant run by the Roux family. Later, Mary discovers an oasis floating on the Thames, when she meets an established river community who have turned their barges into gardens teaming with fruit and vegetables

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the second episode, Nadiya shares recipes for her most indulgent desserts, from frozen blueberry and banana cheesecake to husband's favourite jam roly poly. Later, Nadiya also shares her tips and tricks for her picture perfect tutti frutti pavlova

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Margaret sends for Rasselas as she has a use for him in her feud with Quigley. Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: With their plan to purchase Abigail unravelling, Charlotte is locked up by Lydia. Period drama set in London in the 1760s following Margaret Wells as she struggles to reconcile her roles as mother and brothel owner. Starring Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the third match of the three-game series, which took place at Emirates Old Trafford. Ahead of this series, these nations had faced each other 13 times in the last five years in this format, with England winning 11 of those matches

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part one of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the lack of regulation in the cosmetic surgery industry. The programme examines how potentially risky surgeries have been replaced by non-invasive procedures like injectable fillers and fat freezing, and puts the science and safety of these new approaches to the test. The first episode focuses on facial procedures

programme.title: The Diagnosis Detectives
programme.desc: Michael Mosley challenges 12 of the UK's leading medical experts to make a diagnosis for patients whose debilitating symptoms have baffled other doctors. Paul's dreams of a happy retirement have been ruined by a mystery condition that has caused his face to swell up so dramatically that he sometimes struggles to see, while Judy struggles to eat solid food and is dramatically losing weight

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich set to work transforming a garden in Bitterne, near Southampton, for Katie and Christian and their two children, Max and Olivia

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three treasured family heirlooms - a wooden toolbox, a plate camera and a marionette theatre

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by train through Austria and the Czech Republic, beginning in the northern Austrian city of Linz, where Adolf Hitler lived as a young boy. From here, Michael travels onto Prague Central Station, where he meets 87-year-old Zuzana Maresova, who, as a seven-year-old girl climbed aboard a train to travel to safety in London with hundreds of other Jewish children. Eventually crossing the Czech border into Slovakia, Michael reaches his final destination, one of Europe's youngest capital cities, Bratislava

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin heads to Derby as vintage enthusiast Ryan Goodyear takes on entrepreneur Lorraine Meadowcroft in the antiques-buying challenge

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack has a theory about who was responsible for the accident that put Henry's career in doubt. Anna returns to Ash Park and reveals the identity of the father of her unborn child

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Wildlife film-maker Fergus Beeley travels to the remote Orinoco rainforest of Venezuela to follow the progress of an elusive harpy eagle from birth to adulthood. Renowned as one of the most powerful birds of prey in the world, it is able to pluck monkeys from the branches of the jungle canopy

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose, environmentalist Philippe Cousteau Jr, maritime archaeologist Dr Lucy Blue and marine biologist Tooni Mahto base themselves in the Bahamas to explore the Atlantic. On the island of Andros, they go on a risky dive to discover what the oceans looked like 3.5 billion years ago, and investigate an underwater cave system thought to hold the remains of the first inhabitants

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Paul Laidlaw hunt for antiques in the Bowes Museum at Barnard Castle in Co Durham, with finds including a silver tea set and a Russian drinking cup. Plus, Paul Martin looks at the museum's extraordinary lace collection

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: Coverage of the seventh meeting of the campaign, which takes place at Stadio San Paolo for one season only while Rome's Stadio Olimpico is renovated. Middle distance stars Donavan Brazier and Timothy Cheruiyot, 400m hurdler Karsten Warholm and high jumper Yaroslava Mahuchikh are among the athletes to have made a major impact in this year's series so far. Among the events scheduled to take place at this meeting are the women's and men's 100, 400m and 800m, plus a number of field events such as the high jump and pole vault

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: At Swanage lifeboat station, brothers Matt and Gavin, following in their father's footsteps, race to rescue a pair of novice paddleboarders before they are swept out into the busy shipping lanes. On the north east coast, the Withernsea crew scours the North Sea for a local fisherman and friend who is missing at sea, while in Blackpool, a volunteer's wedding day is interrupted by an urgent call for help

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A further selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. This time around, Michael McIntyre, Kerry Godliman, Kevin Bridges, Sarah Millican, Rhod Gilbert, Shappi Khorsandi, David O'Doherty and Kae Kurd offer their thoughts on shopping

programme.title: Motherland
programme.desc: As the school year begins, a new mum at the gates attracts the attention of Julia, Liz and Kevin, with Amanda telling them she is a high-flying businesswoman with five kids and a sexy husband. Julia is struggling to keep her head above water at work when she is offered the choice of promotion or redundancy. Comedy about the trials and traumas of motherhood, starring Anna Maxwell Martin and Diane Morgan

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tries to help make sense of bewildering times as he dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Joining him this evening are Sara Pascoe, Miles Jupp and Kiri Pritchard-McLean

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: Sketch show, with Freya Parker and Celeste Dring, featuring characters from their live shows, including adventures of a whimsical French heroine, created by a man who has never met a woman

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for brown trout on the river Test in Hampshire, using a Victorian fly-casting technique pioneered in the area as well as an authentic 19th-century rod. The Mayflies are hatching, and the famously short-lived insects inspire some thoughts on the ephemeral nature of life, and what they would do if they only had 24 hours left

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill casts a comical eye over the history of police dramas, from the early days of Dixon of Dock Green to acclaimed modern shows like Line of Duty. He examines the familiar tropes that audiences know and love, as well as drawing attention to a few that have escaped audience's attention

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: The West Tower in the city centre is the latest skyscraper to open to the booming rental market. General manager Matt has over 300 apartments to rent out for owners Legal & General, and starts off with free yoga classes in the penthouse to raise the tower's profile. But as towers rise on the skyline, Salford mayor Paul Dennett needs to find a way to build more affordable housing in the city. Homeless people and low-income earners are often forced into shelters or temporary accommodation as the city's affordable housing stock falls short

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste visit projects to make the countryside more accessible, and discover how a town is coming together to tackle mental health issues

programme.title: Food: Truth or Scare
programme.desc: Chris Bavin and Gloria Hunniford are joined by Edwina Currie as they examine conflicting media stories about the safety of eating eggs, 30 years on from the salmonella scandal

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on a surveyors' theodolite dating back 100 years, a brass bell from a Victorian ice-cream business, and a 60-year-old leather football. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete for the chance to transform the Romsey garden of Harry and Anna, turning it from a boring blank patch into a sophisticated party space

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea uses his expertise to make a dilapidated and unsafe go-kart roadworthy once again. Kirsten Ramsay faces an unusual challenge in the shape of a papier mache cat

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Croydon, south London, teaching actress Anne and government employee Paul a few tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Matt's insistence on finding the mother who gave Georgie up for adoption has unforseen consequences for himself and Olivia. When top surgeon Neil Burton arrives at Inverness Hospital it becomes immediately obvious that he is not there for Henry's job. Anna has a shock for Mrs Collins

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The first of two episodes exploring the Indian Ocean, which accounts for 13 per cent of the Earth's surface and covers 26 million square miles. The expedition visits a 'coral nursery', one of the few places in the world where an extraordinary technique is being used to repair damaged reefs. Paul Rose and the team also head to a small community in Mozambique where shark fishing is an established practice

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral, once home to Benedictine monks. Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some items to take to auction. Among them are an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist. Paul also explores the history of the nearby Broads.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeeper Valentine and market gardener John meet their regular customers and discover new ones selling their skincare products and organic vegetables at St George's weekly market in central Belfast

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: One-time Blue Peter presenters Valerie Singleton and Diane Louise Jordan go searching for the antiques and collectibles they hope will raise them money at auction in Stratford-upon-Avon, with expert help from Paul Laidlaw and Philip Serrell. Along the way, Valerie finds out about the 19th-century process that revolutionised photography, Diane visits a grand country estate that was transformed into a military hospital during the First World War

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With autumn on the horizon, cameras capture the seasonal planting of bulbs for spring-flowering begins at Longmeadow.. Frances Tophill meets a gardener whose allotment is filled with both traditional and exotic vegetables and Toby Buckland visits The Newt in Somerset, to find out about its chequered garden history and to explore its emergence as one of the UK's most exciting gardens. Plus, another chance to see Mark Lane's visit to Oxfordshire to see how ornamental grasses, perennials and annuals can be used in planting design, to create exciting combinations

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian recounts the story of how the Romantics - fuelled by the failures of the French Revolution and horrors of the Industrial Revolution - retreated from the tragic uproar of public life to take a journey into their own minds. Here, long before Freud and Jung and the modern psychological revolution, they discovered the subconscious - something that would have a profound effect on the modern world. Contributors include Tobias Menzies, Peter Doherty, Piotr Anderszewski and David Attenborough

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Despite being fully aware of his limited knowledge of all things botanical, the comedian becomes a florist in Porthcawl. Cutting his teeth during the crazy Valentine's rush, Rhod has days to master this creative skill, before the business competes in the heats of the Chelsea Flower Show

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A water shortage prompts Adama to order the temporary release of prisoners to gather supplies from a deserted ice moon. However, a mutiny results in Galactica officers held hostage by a charismatic rebel questioning the legitimacy of Roslin's government and demanding free elections to be held

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A tragic accident on the Galactica kills 20 pilots, prompting Adama to order a new squad to be trained by Kara, who is still wracked with guilt over the death of the Commander's son years previously. With Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: Fighting the Power: Britain After George Floyd
programme.desc: Daniel Henry follows the Black Lives Matter campaign in the UK, as the country is forced to look again at its record on race in the wake of the death of George Floyd in the US

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: War Walks
programme.desc: Richard Holmes recalls the night of December 29, 1940, when St Paul's Cathedral and much of London suffered heavy bombing, tracing events surrounding the terrible destruction caused during the Blitz

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a majestic forest

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores exciting archaeological finds across the East of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime. Presented by Alice Roberts

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Pilot: The Extraordinary Story of Captain Winkle Brown
programme.desc: Former Royal Navy officer and test pilot Captain Eric 'Winkle' Brown has flown more types of aircraft than anyone in history and is regarded as one of the best pilots Britain has produced. In this programme, the 95-year-old chats to James Holland about his flying experiences and encounters with Nazis, as well as other adventures in the run-up to and during the Second World War, illustrated by archive and personal photographs

programme.title: The Evacuees
programme.desc: Jack Rosenthal's 1975 drama, directed by Alan Parker, chronicling the experiences of two Jewish boys evacuated from London to avoid the horrors of the Blitz. Maureen Lipman, Margery Mason and Ray Mort star

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: In a lively interview with Jeremy Isaacs, film-maker Alan Parker talks about how he operates in Hollywood, violence in the movies, and his working-class childhood in Islington

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his exploration of the artistic history of the northern European region by looking at Sweden's tradition of furniture design

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: Paul Copley narrates the story of Grinling Gibbons, the 17th-century woodcarver who helped restore London to its former glory in the aftermath of the Great Fire. He reveals how Gibbons' masterpieces - created for the likes of Charles II and William of Orange - were held in such high regard he came to be known as 'the Michelangelo of wood'

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a majestic forest

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores exciting archaeological finds across the East of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime. Presented by Alice Roberts

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The team investigates some exciting new developments in astronomy

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints an autumn scene, with a cool breeze blowing as trees reflect off a glistening pond

programme.title: Casablanca (1942)
programme.desc: The American expat owner of a Moroccan nightclub is reunited with his old flame during the Second World War. Now married to a Czech freedom fighter, she needs his help to get them safely out of the country before they are captured by the Nazis - but the former lovers also have to wrestle the temptation to rekindle their simmering romance. Drama, with Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Claude Rains and Paul Henreid

programme.title: Parkinson: The Interviews
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson looks back on his meeting with Ingrid Bergman - an actress he confesses to falling in love with the first time he saw her on screen at his local cinema

programme.title: Hunting the Nazi Gold Train
programme.desc: In August 2015 two amateur researchers declared they had found a lost Nazi train buried in a railway cutting near Walbrzych in Silesia, southern Poland. This followed decades of local folklore that a train, loaded with gold, had vanished after leaving Wroclaw in early 1945. Dan Snow follows the treasure hunters as they embark on the hunt, while also investigating this extraordinary story that leads all the way to Adolf Hitler himself and his secret plans for this remote corner of the Third Reich during the closing months of the Second World War

programme.title: Tornado: The 100mph Steam Engine
programme.desc: Cameras following the fortunes of new A1 class steam engine Tornado as it tries to achieve 100mph on the main line, a secret attempt made in the middle of the night

programme.title: Plague Fiction
programme.desc: In light of the Covid-19 global outbreak, Professor Laura Ashe takes a look back at the Black Death of the 14th century, the deadliest pandemic recorded in human history. Going through the literature of the time, from one of the earliest accounts in 1347, to the great Plague Diary of Samuel Pepys, Professor Ashe explores how literature helped us cope with fear and tragedy, the importance of bravery and personal sacrifice, and whether the words of the past can offer us the comfort and healing that we need now

programme.title: Life Cinematic
programme.desc: The director of American Beauty, Skyfall and Revolutionary Road talks to Edith Bowman about the movies that have influenced his career. His choices range from Blue Velvet, Taxi Driver and The Godfather II to Lost In Translation. Sam also provides an insight into the making of his most recent film, 1917, and reflects on his early introduction to cinema

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers tips and tricks as he paints an autumn scene, with a cool breeze blowing as trees reflect off a glistening pond

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Pilot: The Extraordinary Story of Captain Winkle Brown
programme.desc: Former Royal Navy officer and test pilot Captain Eric 'Winkle' Brown has flown more types of aircraft than anyone in history and is regarded as one of the best pilots Britain has produced. In this programme, the 95-year-old chats to James Holland about his flying experiences and encounters with Nazis, as well as other adventures in the run-up to and during the Second World War, illustrated by archive and personal photographs

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1978
programme.desc: A selection of classic songs from 1978, a year in which punk music and new wave rebellion co-existed with pop, disco, soul, reggae and prog rock. Featuring appearances on the show by Ian Dury, Elvis Costello, Blondie, Bob Marley, the Boomtown Rats, Kate Bush, Sham 69, Brotherhood of Man, Dan Hill, Boney M, 10cc and Manfred Mann's Earth Band

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: How, in the '70s and '80s, a second coming of soul men appealed to black women. New record label Philadelphia International was born that would also soften soul's hard edges. It was home to Harold Melvin and the Blue Notes, saw the emergence of Teddy Pendergrass and incorporated the O'Jays, who embodied a message of love and togetherness. Marvin Gaye went to work on his follow-up to What's Going On, shifting focus from the streets to the bedroom with Let's Get It On. Barry White's deep baritone also put both black and white audiences 'in the mood'. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: Classic Soul at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances from the BBC archive by acclaimed soul singers, including Aretha Franklin, Al Green, Isaac Hayes, Dusty Springfield, Solomon Burke and Percy Sledge

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix: The Road to Woodstock
programme.desc: A definitive account of one of the guitarist's most celebrated performances, at the New York festival in 1969, featuring signature renditions of Purple Haze, Voodoo Child and the Star Spangled Banner. The programme includes interviews with Woodstock promoter Michael Lang and Hendrix's band members Mitch Mitchell, Billy Cox, Larry Lee and Juma Sultan

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The soul and jazz performer examines how early 20th-century blues growlers, such as Bessie Smith, paved the way for the rhyme and flow of hip-hop, how truth became a quest of rock 'n' roll's greatest poets from Woody Guthrie to Gil Scott-Heron, to Lou Reed and Suzanne Vega, and why popular voices including Bob Dylan, Leonard Cohen and Kurt Cobain don't have to be technically perfect to resonate so deeply and stir people's souls. Contributors include Dave Grohl, Suzanne Vega and KRS-One

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: How, in the '70s and '80s, a second coming of soul men appealed to black women. New record label Philadelphia International was born that would also soften soul's hard edges. It was home to Harold Melvin and the Blue Notes, saw the emergence of Teddy Pendergrass and incorporated the O'Jays, who embodied a message of love and togetherness. Marvin Gaye went to work on his follow-up to What's Going On, shifting focus from the streets to the bedroom with Let's Get It On. programme.desc: Messy and his friends go camping in OKIDO Forest and meet some nocturnal animals

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friend play pizza restaurants, and Mr Tumble knits a jumper

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po play a game of hide and seek. They both take turns at hiding

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: Team Tinpo are called out to Tinpotown Airfield, where their effort to get things off the ground inadvertently kicks off a bird b-boy party

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Crunch has a new fairy friend that no one else can see. The Olobobs make Hairy Fairy, who is so bad at magic that Crunch's fairy appears to teach him how it's done

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: The little boy loves Uncle John's box of buttons, but Tang can't resist opening it and letting the buttons out to share the smells, shapes and memories of the places they're from

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo and his friends encourage young viewers to question the world around them

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: Observational documentary on the lives of reception children at two contrasting primary schools - one rural, and one urban

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Mr McGregor locks Peter in a cage with Sammy Whiskers and he must join forces with the rat to escape

programme.title: Maddie, the Zoo and You
programme.desc: Maddie finds out how we can stay clean and comfortable.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog goes back to read the very first book he read - a simple picture book.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: YolanDa's Band Jam
programme.desc: YolanDa Brown and her house band, the Band Jaminals, entertain an audience of children

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gram and Grand visit. Waffle loves Gram; he'll only leave his new dog doormat for her

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran go to the museum to find a giant fossilised Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Scarf Lady welcomes Sarah and Duck into her house, where they discover just how she came by her name

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Dibillo decides to wear his favourite bow tie for the day

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: The froglets are playing chase with Tiny on her scooter, but Small can't run fast enough to keep up. So he makes his own space hopper; but unplanned, it hops him into orbit

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Makka Pakka gets lost and cannot find his way home

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed
