programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a calligrapher from East London find a new home in Devon on a budget of £400,000, and attends a sea shanty festival on the southern coast

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich set to work transforming a garden in Bitterne, near Southampton, for Katie and Christian and their two children, Max and Olivia

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three treasured family heirlooms - a wooden toolbox, a plate camera and a marionette theatre

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Entomologist Sarah Beynon and chef Andy Holcroft embark on a mission to bring insect-based food into the mainstream, promoting it as a more sustainable source of protein than meat. Before pitching to potential buyers, they go to the Netherlands to check that the insect farmers there can supply the quantity and quality of protein that they need and that it is safe for human consumption. Part of the Our Lives strand

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: Shane Williams: Rugby, Concussion and Me
programme.desc: Shane Williams heads on a journey to understand how the world of rugby, from grass roots to the highest levels, is working to reduce concussions in the modern-day game and helping to make the sport safer for future generations. He talks to players past and present, the custodians of the game at World Rugby and leading global medical professionals studying the long-term effects of contact sport on the brain

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail forges a connection with Adil, a new visitor to base camp, and pushes a devastated Raelle to help him save a life. Tally uses Gerit as a distraction from her increasingly burdensome secrets, while Anacostia probes into Scylla's past

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The witches' training culminates at City Drop, their final training exercise. Devastating secrets come to light, splintering the unit and forcing Tally to question her blind trust. Later, a looming Spree threat changes the course of the cadets' lives

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wallasey on Merseyside, Porth in south Wales, and Ripley in Derbyshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A criminal has his Rolex watch seized and the police target counterfeit cigarette sellers in Cleveland, believing large sums of money are being made from unsafe products

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: The housing officers in Liverpool visit a cramped terraced property in a worrying state of repair. In Birmingham, an overgrown property is in danger of taking over the neighbours

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Catherine Southon help teams at Sandown Park Racecourse in Surrey find items of value. Presenter Eric Knowles also visits Milestones Museum in Basingstoke

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie fears a young evacuee is in danger, while a death leaves Joyce reeling. Lady Ellen alarms her jealous sister when she shows her warm side, and Jack tries to persuade Bea that Chicago is the safest place for her and the baby. Esther decides to take drastic action to raise the money needed to save Martin's eyesight

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple returning from 20 years living in America as they look for a home in Hampshire's New Forest near to their children's new school. Sonali also goes behind the scenes during the preparations for a hugely popular traditional country show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete for the chance to transform the Romsey garden of Harry and Anna, turning it from a boring blank patch into a sophisticated party space

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on a surveyors' theodolite dating back 100 years, a brass bell from a Victorian ice-cream business, and a 60-year-old leather football. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show with Alex Jones and Ronan Keating, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle goes to the seaside cafe to ring Kheerat only to get a shock when she returns to the B&B. Ian tightens the reins on Sharon running the Vic, and Callum asks Ben if the offer to move in together is still on the table - but their happy moment is short-lived. Stuart and Rainie prepare for their stag and hen parties, although things do not exactly go to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for the cops on the island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. However, it's all change for the team. DI Jack Mooney (Ardal O'Hanlon) is now in charge, and his first Caribbean case sees the reopening of a seven-year-old murder when a tourist provides an alibi for the woman convicted of the crime

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view a chalet bungalow in East Peckham, Kent, a piece of land in Tredegar, south-east Wales, and an end-terrace in Hartshill, Warwickshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in North Yorkshire look for county lines drug dealers in Harrogate, while at the auction one fraudster is about to have their home sold to pay back the money they made illegally. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie tries to win Henry's respect by standing up to the overbearing bishop, and Finch is angry to learn Esther received money for Martin's operation. Fed up with the Land Army, Joyce forces Tucker into an admission of guilt, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana is part of Jack's plan to escape with Bea and the baby. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Liam Garrigan and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes from their favourite cookery books, explaining their value as social documents that reveal a great deal about people and what they ate. The duo meet Ivan Day, who has one of the most extensive collections of recipe books in the UK, and share stories of renowned food writers such as Elizabeth David and Isabella Beeton

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Precious metal expert Brenton West takes on a pewter chess set representing the War of Independence, complete with 32 figures of British and American soldiers. A Syrian chair and a wartime mascot doll are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Riyadh Khalaf host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: Wales once led the world in the production of coal, exporting the black gold to the four corners of the Earth. Since that industry collapsed much of the infrastructure has been destroyed, forgotten or hidden away. Here, the adventurer goes in search of those final pieces of coal history, visiting one of the country's last coal mines, where they have dug up centuries' old workings. Will also explores the abandoned buildings of some south Wales forgotten collieries and lands on an island that was responsible for wrecking hundreds of coal ships

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Chantelle arrive back in Walford, but despite her finally finding the courage to tell her husband she is leaving him, Chantelle is unaware that the silent horror she has lived with for many years is about to get even worse

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a scout leader, and is put in charge of leading a large group of excitable 12-year-olds safely through a forest on a wilderness survival camp. The real test, however, is whether or not Rhod will be able to bite his tongue when his tent comes under attack at 7am

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Adventure, recounted through Tonto's memories of the earliest efforts from himself and the Lone Ranger to subdue local villainy and bring justice to the American Old West. Johnny Depp, Armie Hammer, Tom Wilkinson and Helena Bonham Carter star

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Precious metal expert Brenton West takes on a pewter chess set representing the War of Independence, complete with 32 figures of British and American soldiers. A Syrian chair and a wartime mascot doll are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Riyadh Khalaf host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Beinn Eighe National Nature Reserve in Wester Ross to find out what has changed, as visitors return to such rural areas after lockdown. He also learns how a genetically distinct ancient pine forest is being regenerated for the future. Euan McIlwraith discovers how Scotland's wildlife from Mull to Shetland is thriving this year, Arlene Stuart finds out who picked the fruit and vegetable crops this season, and Anne Lundon follows the epic journey of Glasgow's water supply

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Chantelle arrive back in Walford, but despite her finally finding the courage to tell her husband she is leaving him, Chantelle is unaware that the silent horror she has lived with for many years is about to get even worse

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A hypnotist is booked to provide the entertainment at the triplets' baptism - and Agnes soon finds herself under his spell. Meanwhile, the matriarch's kitchen is falling apart - but that turns out to be the least of her problems when son Mark delivers some heartbreaking news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Two terraces near beautiful countryside in Prestatyn in north Wales and Darley Dale in the Peak District and a shop and flat in Folkestone in Kent are all sold under the hammer. All three properties need a lot of work and Martin Roberts, Dion Sublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new investors to hear their plans

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: National Crime Agency officers seize cash and drugs from a suspected drug dealer, and Border Force officers at Gatwick sell off a huge haul of gold seized from a passenger

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright heads to Liverpool where council housing officers deal with the case of a family who are struggling to cope with a landlord they claim is intrusive

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Gary Pe and Nick Hall give advice to two teams scouring Ardingly in West Sussex for items to sell at auction, where one of Willy Wonka's golden tickets goes under the hammer

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie discovers more about the piano player she is in love with. Joyce realises there is something wrong with Martin and decides to follow him. Elsewhere, Finch helps Esther raise money by setting up a boxing match with his old adversary Vernon. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Susan Cookson and Mykola Allen

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a calligrapher from East London find a new home in Devon on a budget of £400,000, and attends a sea shanty festival on the southern coast

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and brothers Harry and David Rich set to work transforming a garden in Bitterne, near Southampton, for Katie and Christian and their two children, Max and Olivia

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore three treasured family heirlooms - a wooden toolbox, a plate camera and a marionette theatre

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood examines bogus Nectar cards, and fake fire resistant glass that was installed in schools, hospitals and churches around the UK

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Filmed in the autumn of 2019, cameras follow a new cast of characters from the London Ambulance Service as they care for the nine million people of the city. In the first episode, ambulance crew members Kate and Femi are dispatched to a young man who is struggling to breathe during a severe anxiety attack. Meanwhile, just three hours into the morning shift, control deal with the third assault of the day, having already responded to four stabbings

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an exciting audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Abigail forges a connection with Adil, a new visitor to base camp, and pushes a devastated Raelle to help him save a life. Tally uses Gerit as a distraction from her increasingly burdensome secrets, while Anacostia probes into Scylla's past

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The witches' training culminates at City Drop, their final training exercise. Devastating secrets come to light, splintering the unit and forcing Tally to question her blind trust. Later, a looming Spree threat changes the course of the cadets' lives

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Wallasey on Merseyside, Porth in south Wales, and Ripley in Derbyshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: A criminal has his Rolex watch seized and the police target counterfeit cigarette sellers in Cleveland, believing large sums of money are being made from unsafe products

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: The housing officers in Liverpool visit a cramped terraced property in a worrying state of repair. In Birmingham, an overgrown property is in danger of taking over the neighbours

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Catherine Southon help teams at Sandown Park Racecourse in Surrey find items of value. Presenter Eric Knowles also visits Milestones Museum in Basingstoke

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie fears a young evacuee is in danger, while a death leaves Joyce reeling. Lady Ellen alarms her jealous sister when she shows her warm side, and Jack tries to persuade Bea that Chicago is the safest place for her and the baby. Esther decides to take drastic action to raise the money needed to save Martin's eyesight

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how Mediterranean cuisine and ingredients have influenced British culinary culture since it was first introduced by the Romans, and prepare their favourite recipes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple returning from 20 years living in America as they look for a home in Hampshire's New Forest near to their children's new school. Sonali also goes behind the scenes during the preparations for a hugely popular traditional country show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete for the chance to transform the Romsey garden of Harry and Anna, turning it from a boring blank patch into a sophisticated party space

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on a surveyors' theodolite dating back 100 years, a brass bell from a Victorian ice-cream business, and a 60-year-old leather football. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show with Alex Jones and Ronan Keating, featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chantelle goes to the seaside cafe to ring Kheerat only to get a shock when she returns to the B&B. Ian tightens the reins on Sharon running the Vic, and Callum asks Ben if the offer to move in together is still on the table - but their happy moment is short-lived. Stuart and Rainie prepare for their stag and hen parties, although things do not exactly go to plan

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Another mystery for the cops on the island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. However, it's all change for the team. DI Jack Mooney (Ardal O'Hanlon) is now in charge, and his first Caribbean case sees the reopening of a seven-year-old murder when a tourist provides an alibi for the woman convicted of the crime

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Adam Fleming and Fergus Walsh present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: One-off edition of the observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. The programmes examines how long-serving and newly-qualified Northumbria Police officers respond to the changing world of modern crime, including managing online sex offenders and the threat of firearms. In one of Northumbria's newest departments - the Paedophile Online Investigation Team - Detective Constable Dave Kernaghan is managing a huge rise in new cases, but as he continues to investigate the escalating number of jobs, the work itself takes a toll as he assesses the impact this is having on his own life

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view a chalet bungalow in East Peckham, Kent, a piece of land in Tredegar, south-east Wales, and an end-terrace in Hartshill, Warwickshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in North Yorkshire look for county lines drug dealers in Harrogate, while at the auction one fraudster is about to have their home sold to pay back the money they made illegally. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates a Surrey property overwhelmed with rubbish and talks to the owner about their struggles with hoarding. In west London, probate genealogist Richard tracks down a deceased homeowner's relatives to help them claim their inheritance

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie tries to win Henry's respect by standing up to the overbearing bishop, and Finch is angry to learn Esther received money for Martin's operation. programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Reeling from Des' change in plea, Jay and the police throw themselves into proving premeditation, hoping to show to the court he was of sound mind when he committed his crimes. As the trial begins and the defence start to tear strips off key witnesses, Jay and his team are shocked by just how flimsy their case against Nilsen seems to be. Could this seemingly open-and-shut murder investigation really be close to collapsing?

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera

Plus, the touching moment that a baby hears her big sister for the first time and an incredible farewell flashmob for a much-loved headteacher in Greater Manchester

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: For rubber baron Walter Blackett and his family, life in 1941 Singapore is full of wealth and privilege, where the horrors of war-torn Europe are a distant whisper. But when their power and stability are rocked by new arrivals and the looming threat of a Japanese invasion, Walter has a cunning plan. Adaptation of the novel by Booker Prize-winner JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Charles Dance, Jane Horrocks and Colm Meaney

programme.title: Des
programme.desc: Reeling from Des' change in plea, Jay and the police throw themselves into proving premeditation, hoping to show to the court he was of sound mind when he committed his crimes. As the trial begins and the defence start to tear strips off key witnesses, Jay and his team are shocked by just how flimsy their case against Nilsen seems to be. Could this seemingly open-and-shut murder investigation really be close to collapsing?

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The travelling chef arrives in the Texan city of Fort Worth, famous for its open-range cattle drives of the late 19th century. James gets in the spirit and tries his hand at cattle roping, before cooking outside the cattle exchange. He also dusts off his boots to take part in line dancing at the world's largest honky tonk bar, then discovers a riverside oasis to barbecue a few Texan meats

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: For rubber baron Walter Blackett and his family, life in 1941 Singapore is full of wealth and privilege, where the horrors of war-torn Europe are a distant whisper. But when their power and stability are rocked by new arrivals and the looming threat of a Japanese invasion, Walter has a cunning plan. Adaptation of the novel by Booker Prize-winner JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Charles Dance, Jane Horrocks and Colm Meaney

programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. programme.desc: Biff urges Jan to think again about taking Kim's job offer. Zak tries to explain to Terry why he went missing the night before. Chris and Rachel celebrate their divorce

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf celebrates his 70th birthday. Tricia tries to make Jamie see they are better off without Carl. Raquel is furious when Curly lets her down over an evening out

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura travels to Edinburgh to try to uncover the truth behind Andrew's wife's death, but finds more than she bargained for. Meanwhile, tensions between Tom and Katy reach breaking point as their secret threatens to surface. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernon's plans for Liz and Ben's wedding in chaos, while Helen's dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is upset that Jan knew about Biff's possible illness. Sophie admits to Zoe that she is scared of Butch. Tony gets the blackmail money back from Adlington

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is worried about Butch as he did not come home the night before. PC Wilson assesses the damage to Zoe's living room. Chris sees Kim and Steve kissing in her bedroom

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud urges Maureen to make peace with Bill. Alf demands Audrey cancels the health farm trip. Curly is angry to discover Raquel is trying to sort out his problems with the Dealers

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia gets a letter from Carl blaming her for him losing his parole. Sue calls on Ken and opens her heart to him about her husband leaving

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al welcomes his first fishing guests, but an uninvited one means that a good review is definitely not guaranteed, while Bert decides to try to rejuvenate trade at the restaurant by joining an online voucher scheme. Morwenna asks Martin for a pay rise, and Louisa has to find a new babysitter to look after James Henry and reluctantly decides to offer Janice a trial. Penhale is excited to receive his new police issue Taser, but Portwenn does not exactly offer him plenty of opportunities to try it out. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's resolve to bring Andrew to justice deepens and she enlists Tom's help in her plan. However, when a deception in her personal life comes to light, she is left with no one to turn to and resorts to dangerous methods to force Andrew's confession. But she is not alone in her mission. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt, Ioan Gruffudd and Warren Brown

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Terrified of being arrested, Andrew enlists Luke's help to set up an alibi, but Laura does not give up easily and the final confrontation between the two may have violent consequences. The concluding part of Harry and Jack Williams' thriller, which followed the lives of teacher Laura Nielson and widowed surgeon Andrew Earlham after they found themselves entangled in a web of deceit and confusion after enjoying a date together. Starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Danny Webb and Shelley Conn

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bill apologises to Maureen, thinking he has embarrassed her. Don is furious when he hears Nick is living in Canada. Tricia visits Carl in prison

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Andy cannot face going to school. Chris takes out his problems on Sophie. The police question the Dingles about a robbery in the village. Biff provides Kathy with some comfort

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Roy tells Biff he saw him with Kathy, and Biff tries to tell Linda about the incident with Kathy. Becky attempts to get Dean's dad to drop the charges

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The police investigate the murder of an ex-army recluse and archaeological enthusiast and suspect a building dispute could provide a motive, while Dennis asks Liz for advice when he begins to notice a change in Jenny's behaviour. Vernon decides to play Cupid on behalf of his relative Gareth and sets him up on a blind date with Rosie, but does not count on the mechanic having another admirer. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Aislin McGuckin and Vanessa Hehir

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid has problems when Jean pleads for a new freezer

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton investigates the murder of a retired policeman who was about to flee the village for Spain and, after visiting the victim's widow, discovers the man kept more than a few secrets from his wife. Vernon decides to arrange a poker night in the hope it will make him easy money, but doesn't count on playing against a hustler. Rural drama starring Duncan Bell, Geoffrey Hughes and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is determined not to have Andy put off going to school because of Viv's demonstration. Zoe asks Sophie to spy for her at Kim's party

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim hopes to be discreet about her relationship with Steve. Eric is jumpy when he spots Kathy looking through her accounts. Sophie tells Butch that he is being pathetic

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Andy moves out of number 11. Audrey convinces herself Nick is better off with Stephen than with Gail. The Duckworths comfort Tricia as she breaks down in tears over her black eye.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is surprised by Raquel's new positive attitude. Jim teases Bill about his relationship with Maureen. The police ask Tricia to press charges against Carl

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: David Jason returns as DI Jack Frost in this feature-length episode. Faced with the death of a police informant on an estate riven with racial tension, the detective finds his patience pushed to the limit. Bruce Alexander and Lennie James also star

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's worst fears come to pass and her hidden past is exposed, but she must fight to put her personal struggles aside for the sake of one of her pupils, whose physical distress throws her unexpectedly at the mercy of Andrew Earlham. Vanessa also finds her resolve shaken after an unsettling encounter with Andrew

programme.desc: A bad dream has Regan's girlfriend fearing for his safety, and her visions ring true when he gets involved in a case involving a stolen lorryload of cigars. programme.title: The Living Daylights (1987)
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik

programme.title: Scarface (1983)
programme.desc: Small-time crook Tony Montana emigrates from Cuba to the US, determined to become rich and powerful. Admitted into the inner circle of a Miami drug lord, he ultimately takes over his boss's empire and even his wife. However, his paranoia and cocaine addiction begin to spiral out of control, while his enemies circle, waiting for the chance to bring him down. Gangster thriller remake, with Al Pacino, Michelle Pfeiffer and Robert Loggia

programme.title: World Cup Top Goalscorers

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 19, featuring a flat 166.5km course from Bourg-en-Bresse to Champagnole. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: World Series of Darts Finals
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening day of the tournament, staged at the Salzburgarena in Austria, where Michael van Gerwen is the defending champion. The Dutchman is the top seed in the field of 24 players, with the likes of Peter Wright, Gerwyn Price and Michael Smith among the other favourites. This is the sixth staging of the event, and van Gerwen has been victorious on all but one occasion, when James Wade claimed the title in 2018. Presented by Jacqui Oatley, with analysis by Chris Mason and Alan Warriner-Little, and commentary by John Rawling and Stuart Pyke

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A report from Southeast Asia, of something in the water that's sliced cleanly through a young man's testicles, is enough to lure 'fishing detective' Jeremy Wade to the shores of the Mekong - a river long renowned for its monstrous fish. But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims who've been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating

programme.desc: Richard Henwood presents action from obscure motorsports, such as bus and caravan racing and ice speedway

programme.desc: An episode following the stories of fathers and sons and the bonds between them. John is rushed into St George's with a life threatening abdominal aortic aneurism. As the doctors work out whether they can save the 77-year-old's life with emergency surgery, his only son discusses the prospect of losing his father. Elsewhere, cameras follow Jamie, whose motorbike collided with a car on a motorway slip road on his way to work, and carpenter Noel arrives in A&E after partially amputating his thumb

programme.title: Lost at Sea: My Dad's Last Journey
programme.desc: Following Louis Bird's journey to piece together the life of his father, Peter Bird, after he disappeared on a voyage at sea in 1996. In 1983, Peter Bird was the first person to row the Pacific Ocean single-handed. Crossing 8,000 treacherous miles in a row-boat, turned Peter into an international legend. On a 1996 voyage, he disappeared, and his empty boat was recovered with a film camera still attached, but he was never found. This programme follows Louis on a personal journey to understand his father, as he and his mother come to terms with feelings of loss and abandonment

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: On St Mary's, Charlotte Hicks returns to the place of her birth to become the Isles of Scilly's only midwife, and the first mum due to give birth off the Cornish coast is her childhood friend Meriel King. At the edge of Dartmoor National Park, woodman John Williamson takes on new apprentice Jordan Harris, and Torquay's harbour master Nick Burns needs to replace a 140-metre section of harbour that will help protect the millions of pounds worth of yachts berthing in the winter months

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: The work of council workers in Southwark, south London, one of the biggest housing associations in the country. The officers tackle fly-tipping, including 40 tons of rubbish dumped on council land that had been cleared for building new homes. An ageing estate is planned for demolition, meaning 10,000 residents are moving - but it's a sad day for many, including a couple leaving their family home of 50 years. And hoarding manager Laurna helps a scholar whose thousands of books have overtaken his home

programme.title: When Cruises Go Wrong
programme.desc: In a typical tourist season, around two million British people will take to the seas for the holiday of a lifetime promising class, elegance and refinement. However, as this documentary reveals, it's not always plain sailing. Cameras capture the downside of cruising, from sickness and crime to terrifying weather, collisions and emergency evacuations

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sandra Dee's, a seafood and barbecue restaurant in desperate need of a renovation in Sacramento, California. His team have just 24 hours to refurbish the establishment, and Gordon creates an exciting new menu by incorporating existing dishes and elevating them to a higher standard. Will returning customers be impressed by the changes?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three restaurants he helped that were among the biggest challenges he faced, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he meets sisters Rita and Lisa to see if they have continued serving fresh instead of frozen food at Boston's La Galleria 33, before heading to the Olde Hitching Post in Hanson, Massachusetts, to discover whether controlling owner Tom has kept his promise and handed over the reins to his daughter Andrea. Finally, Gordon goes back to the Prohibition gastro pub in Everett, Washington, to catch up with owner Rishi and learn if she has ditched belly-dancing in favour of running her business

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Nottinghamshire, Nena and her husband Vish kick off the competition with an Indian feast. Gym-loving guests Syreeta and Romaine are worried the food will be too spicy, while final couple Paul and wife Vicki think the menu is nothing special. Day two sees Syreeta and laid-back Romaine host an evening. The post-meal planking contest proves a hit, but Romaine's romantic crooning hits a bum note. Finally it's the turn of Vicki and her husband Paul to try to see off the competition and bag themselves the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: The chances of a 25 to 35-year-old person owning a home have halved in the last 20 years, while millions of over-sixties are sitting on empty rooms, so here young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. In this third instalment, dog groomer Liah-Rose moves home and business to Reading and pensioner landlords Carolynne and David's high-octane lifestyle. Meanwhile, Jake from Middlesbrough heads for 61-year-old Christene's place in Pontefract, West Yorkshire

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Anna and the team from the Rhino Revolution dehorn two rare and very dangerous black rhinos, while Emma prepares Finya the black-backed jackal for release. Jenna and Sean, the leading forces in elephant research, tackle the growing problem of human and elephant conflict

programme.title: Can We Cure Kids' Cancer?
programme.desc: Following three children and their families as they undergo both cutting-edge and traditional treatments at the Royal Marsden in London. Cameras follow three-year-old Charlotte, who is one of the first children in the UK to take a pioneering new targeted drug that has saved her life, while toddler Artemis must undergo a stem cell transplant, first done over 50 years ago, as this is the best option available to her. Elsewhere Lily, who is 14, takes on a cocktail of chemotherapy and newer immunotherapy drugs

programme.title: Fifteen to One
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the general knowledge quiz in which 15 contestants try to win a place in the end-of-series final, when a £40,000 jackpot will be up for grabs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty visits Europe's biggest brewery - Heineken in the Netherlands - on a mission to explain the difference between lager and ale. His journey takes a surprising twist when he comes face to face with the mother of all lagers. Kate Quilton sets Matt Tebbutt the challenge of making crumpets, and finds out how they get filled with unique holes at the Warburtons factory in Enfield, north London. Matt also heads for snowy Canada to discover why maple syrup is so expensive, and finds out that maple syrup robberies are a big concern to the industry

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Guest Steph McGovern is alongside Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner for the long-running words-and-numbers game, presented by Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: After being shot during a robbery, Mike decides he needs to live every day as though it's his last, and he tells Carl that the time is right for him to quit the police force. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: After re-reading the first draft of her novel, Molly realises that she hates it - so she decides to take a break from writing, by taking a mindless job at Vince's warehouse. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: The bar-room staff and regulars are bequeathed $100,000 by a former customer, but their new-found wealth soon causes friction. Classic comedy, starring Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Carla asks Sam to be her 'plus one' for her ex-husband Nick's wedding to his glamorous new flame, but Sam's appearance makes Nick so jealous that he begs Carla to take him back. Comedy, with Ted Danson and Rhea Perlman

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Amy's brother Peter turns to Ray for advice on talking to women, while Debra decides to help out by throwing a party and inviting all her single friends

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra nags Raymond to tidy up after himself, but he is more concerned with a letter he has received from his sporting hero - which accidentally ends up in the bin

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: An offhand comment to the twins' new friend has dire consequences for Ray, who cannot understand why everyone is so upset - but he compounds his error by accidentally repeating the remark to the boy's father. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles and Frasier befriend Kirby, whose girlfriend's grandfather is a well-known wine connoisseur. Martin is forced to wear a device to monitor his blood pressure. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Seattle shrink's agent Bebe puts her lowly assistant in charge of his career when a popular new radio psychiatrist appears on the scene. Meanwhile, Martin seems unwilling to make a doctor's appointment. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Shane Evans, COO and co-founder of massage therapy chain Massage Heights, poses as a contestant on a TV reality show who is trying fund her own business. After spending nine years moulding the company, she decides to find out first-hand how her retreats are doing so she can fix potential problems before expanding the business to 300 new locations

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef again comes to the aid of failing American restaurants, starting in Fishtown, Philadelphia, where he tries to help the three owners of the Hot Potato Cafe, which has not made a profit in two years. Spurred on by a scathing review by an influential critic, he makes radical changes to alter the staff's attitudes and create food that is up to his own exacting standards

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Direct from Leeds, Steph McGovern presents a lively mix of celebrity guests, fantastic food, intriguing real-life features and the best entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, the One O'Clock Views feature

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After many years of hard work, Debbie and Nick from Salford believe the time is right to buy a holiday home close to the traditional Spanish town of Dénia on the Costa Blanca. Spectacular views at the top of the couple's wish list, so armed with a budget of £150,000, Laura Hamilton sets out to line up five options for them to consider

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, three rival couples compete to host the best dinner party and win £1,000. Married couple Michelle and Laura kick off the competition with a hearty yet elegant menu. On day two Rob and Emma up the ante with locally sourced produce that's 'proper' dinner party food, before Lorenzo and Katie put on generous Italian feast for the final night

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Former US president George Bush Sr and his wife Barbara buy a house across the street from the family, but Homer and Bart soon cross swords with him and a full-scale feud ensues - one which is not only about politics

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family visits an exclusive country club, but its swanky atmosphere has a disturbing effect on Marge, forcing Homer to bring her back down to earth. Featuring the guest voice of golfer Tom Kite

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: With the majority of people under 30 struggling to get on the property ladder, and millions of over-sixties sitting on empty rooms, young and old are tackling Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. This time, Cairo, who was homeless for several years, leaves his temporary accommodation to stay with 66-year-old in-line skater Bonnie, based in Kensington and Chelsea. Meanwhile, Lucy moves in with Viv and Merv in Norfolk, as the 25-year-old tests out lodging as an alternative to living in a camper van

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics return to share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions and the lively discussions that are prompted by the week's biggest and best shows

programme.title: Five Guys a Week
programme.desc: Essex-based photographer Chris, haulage guy Lou, shopping centre manager Adam, analytics manager Kris and oven cleaning technician Michael move in with Tara, a 32-year-old business consultant from Halifax in West Yorkshire, each hoping to win her affections. Nearly all of Tara's friends are married, and just about the most serious relationship she had to date has been with Kaiser - her beloved German shepherd. Top of Tara's list of requirements is a man who can make her laugh. Eventually, five become one - will Tara make the right choice?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Singletons hoping to pair up over dinner include healthcare assistant Anthony, who wants to put his wild past behind him. Is speech therapist Lawrence 'the one'? Wedding singer Georgina has a blind date with property developer Adam, who prides himself on knowing how to treat a woman, while receptionist Bev sits down with former bank manager Howard, who is strangely familiar

programme.title: The Drop (2014)
programme.desc: A Brooklyn bartender secretly provides a service delivering shipments of money to local gangsters. His criminal connections lead to his involvement in a robbery that goes disastrously wrong and digs up the long-buried secrets from his neighbourhood's past. Crime drama based on a short story by Dennis Lehane, starring Tom Hardy, Noomi Rapace and James Gandolfini in his final film role

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Receptionist Anna Gibbons hosts the first party in Southampton, but her burlesque theme does not go down well with her guests. Food snob Greg Bell criticises Anna's decision to use packet pastry, but designer Darren Miller lightens the mood when he joins in with the evening's pole-dancing entertainment

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kitchen designer Darren Miller hosts the second dinner in Southampton, throwing a 1990s rave-themed party. Despite feeling unwell, he ploughs ahead with preparations, but things get off to a bad start when Greg Bell decides to ignore the event's dress code. An unfortunately placed tattoo appears to put the diners off Darren's main course, and a mouldy raspberry found in the dessert looks set to ruin his chance of winning the competition

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Customer service adviser Greg Bell hosts the third dinner party of the week in Southampton, determined to educate his guests in the art of fine dining. programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Tax officer and vegetarian Bijal Mehta hosts the final night of the competition in Southampton, and hopes to create the ultimate meat-free dinner - but some of her guests are disappointed by the menu. Following the meal, the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced

programme.title: Atlantic: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Stretching from the Antarctic in the south to the Arctic in the north, the Atlantic Ocean is vast, wild and unforgiving and each season brings new challenges for its indigenous creatures. Atlantic winters bring bitter storms, suffering and conflict. Animals that spend the frozen months here must be ready to face its fierce onslaught as short days make it difficult to find enough food in time. However, while most are battling to survive, some are just starting to raise a family

programme.title: Walking Britain's Roman Roads
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores Britain's Roman roads, beginning with the country's oldest and longest, which runs from the Kent coast to Shropshire and passes through key sites of Emperor Claudius' invasion and Boudicca's rebellion

programme.title: Nelson: Britain's Great Naval Hero
programme.desc: A fresh look at the remarkable rise of Horatio Nelson, uncovering the scandals, military failures and secrets behind the Battle of Trafalgar's most famous Admiral. The film explores how the establishment of the day sought to make this complex character a symbol of maritime superiority, but also asks what sort of country Nelson was fighting for, at a time when the Royal Navy was Britain's front line of defence and protecting interests around the globe, including the barbaric Atlantic slave trade

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the animals that thrive in inner-city environments across the country. Urban foxes did not exist in Britain until the 1930s, but now these animals inhabit 90% of towns and cities and take residence in built-up living quarters. Meanwhile, cameras observe families of hedgehogs thriving in unkempt gardens, and peregrine falcons nesting in some on London's tallest buildings

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Elderly patient Brian arrives concerned that his feet have turned an alarming shade of blue. But Dr Williams seems more worried about his low mood. A man arrives after an episode of abdominal pain so severe it made him vomit and seek help in A&E the night before, while a woman is suffering insomnia, which in turn may be triggering depression. Nurse Rosie issues a warning to patient Pat when her blood tests reveal her sugar levels have skyrocketed and Dr Arif treats a baby with constipation

programme.title: How to Add 20k to Your Home
programme.desc: Britain's best-loved TV property and interior experts share the secrets of adding value to a property in the most budget-friendly way possible. Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Linda Barker, Scarlette Douglas, Laura Hamilton and Gary McCausland present a number of projects, including converting a garage into a bedroom or cinema room, installing a home office in the garden, and creating an open-plan living space by knocking down walls between kitchens and sitting rooms. What's more, with each idea Craig Phillips explores the DIY route, working out how much the viewer can do themselves to save money - and which bits they should leave to the professionals

programme.title: Extreme Nightmare Tenants
programme.desc: In Birmingham, tenant Jason is living in atrocious conditions. Struggling with no light or heat or running water, he has made a desperate plea to the council to help him. Housing enforcement officer Steve is on a mission to investigate - but will he be able to track down the absent landlord? If not, Jason will face becoming homeless

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Bogus Journey (1991)
programme.desc: The would-be heavy metal superstars face the Grim Reaper as they try to reclaim their souls from Hell, return to the land of the living and stop their robot doppelgangers from changing history. Along the way, they must also master their instruments, so they can triumph at the Battle of the Bands. Comedy sequel, starring Keanu Reeves, Alex Winter, William Sadler, Joss Ackland and George Carlin

However, the agents' personal plans are all interrupted as, one-by-one, they realise errors have been made in closing their latest investigation. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An American couple are attacked in Iraq, in the very same spot a group of US Special Ops snipers were ambushed six months earlier. Determined to get to the bottom of the case, Gibbs is forced to speak to Aaron Davis, the only survivor of the first assault, who is now struggling through PTSD at the Walter Reed Medical Centre. The 300th episode of the military crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Great Scientists
programme.desc: Allan Chapman presents a profile of the 20th century's most celebrated scientist, Albert Einstein. His ideas were central to the development of many of today's technologies, including space travel, mobile phones, digital television and the atomic bomb

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: A pink mansion in Edinburgh gets a makeover from Ann Maurice and Alistair Appleton. They attempt to give the garishly painted home a more traditional Scottish feel by having it professionally redecorated and toning down the dark, oppressive wooden ceiling over the hallway

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team

programme.title: Traffic Cops

In Halifax, a criminal's wellbeing becomes officers' primary concern after they discover a trail of blood at the scene of an apparent drink-related crash

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: New Teacher, New Nightmare (2018)
programme.desc: Following the suspected suicide of her friend and fellow teacher, Julie Taylor is hired to take over a new class. As she contends with recent events, she becomes increasingly concerned her teenager daughter is mixing with dubious company, in particular the school's resident mean girl. In due course Julie's concerns mount as she fears for her daughter's safety. Thriller, starring Julie Taylor, Allie MacDonald and Nia Roam

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica's relationship finally becomes public knowledge to all but Ross, who is starting to get his life back on track by renting a flat opposite the girls. Comedy, starring Courteney Cox and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross makes a bad impression on his new neighbours, and Monica piles the pressure on Chandler, demanding more commitment than he feels able to give. Joey's latest girlfriend proves a little too fiery - but he is afraid to end their relationship

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Shane's therapy session goes well, Nicolette and Chloe are on a high from their secret fun and Rose blames a mistake of hers on Mackenzie

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Tane, Ari and Mackenzie are in a race against time to find Bella and Nikau, while Dean is in a state of shock, and his relationship with Ziggy comes under strain

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: M&S: The Food Factor
programme.desc: For more than a century, Marks & Spencer has been one of the leading high-street stores, but these days it is under attack from all sides, with budget stores selling cheaper clothes, supermarkets upping the quality of their foods and up-to-date fashions available online. Originally shown on the eve of the company's 2018 report being published, Fiona Phillips speaks to shoppers and former staff to see what can be done to restore the store's fortunes

programme.title: Secret Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian's latest Scottish tour covers Falkirk and the Lothians. She takes a canal boat trip that includes a look behind the scenes at the world's only rotating boat lift. Venturing into Midlothian, she visits a royal hideaway with a dramatic history and tries her hand at falconry. She also visits the site of the world stone-stacking championship and learns the secrets of a small island with links to smuggling that is believed to have inspired Treasure Island

programme.title: Atlantic: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: As winter draws to a close, the Atlantic springs back to life and hope returns. Temperatures rise, days lengthen and food is plentiful again. However, spring in the north is short, the animals only have a few months to find food, a mate and start a family - all the while avoiding predators. Around the British and Irish coast, thousands of gannets that spent winter off southern Europe and north Africa, must find a place to nest before they can raise a family. Meanwhile, killer whales leave Iceland and head to the Shetland Isles, where they hunt harbour seal pups

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Good Morning Britain co-host Kate Garraway narrates this showcase of unforgettable moments involving TV guests. Gloria Hunniford tells the story of when Bruce Forsyth went walkabout on her chat show, ex-cricketer Monty Panesar relives his disastrous appearance on Celebrity Mastermind, while Jenni Falconer remembers her embarrassing encounter with David Hasselhoff, which caused an audience of millions to cringe. Plus, Star Trek actor William Shatner is seen at his most bizarre. Other less-than-perfect moments involve the likes of Phillip Schofield, Jonathan Ross and legendary punk rock hell-raiser John Lydon
